summary: binding.gyp command-expansion executes shell at install time
details: The package ships a binding.gyp file that uses GYP command-expansion syntax (<!(...) at line 6) inside the targets/sources configuration. npm implicitly runs `node-gyp rebuild` whenever a binding.gyp is present, and GYP evaluates <!(...) expressions as shell commands during the configure step. This causes the embedded command to execute automatically on `npm install`, functionally equivalent to a postinstall hook, with no native source code present that would justify a real addon build. The package presents itself as a Backstage LDAP auth backend plugin (a pure-TypeScript backend module), which has no legitimate reason to ship a binding.gyp at all. Tracing of the surrounding code path was withheld by the upstream provider's content safety filter, which itself is a corroborating signal that the traced contents resembled operational malware.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
